title: veronica austriaca var. dubia
intro: Veronica austriaca var. dubia, also known as Austrian speedwell or Persian speedwell, is a perennial plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of iridoid glycosides. The health benefits of Veronica austriaca var. dubia include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, tea, or infusion to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be cautious and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
